question: An angle of intersection of the curves, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is :
solution:
Given equations are x2a2+y2 b2=1 ...1 and x2+y2=ab.....2 where  a>b.

From 1,

b2x2+a2y2=a2b2

Substituting value of y2 from 2 in the above equation

b2x2+a2ab-x2=a2b2

⇒b2-a2x2=a2b2-a3b
⇒x2=ba2b-ab2-a2=a2ba+b

∴x=a2ba+b

Substituting value of x2 in 2, we get

y2=ab2a+b

∴y=ab2a+b
Point of intersection is a2ba+b, ab2a+b

Now, from 1,
x2a2+y2b2=1

⇒2xa2+2yb2dydx=0
⇒dydx=m1=-b2xa2y

From 2,
x2+y2=ab

⇒2x+2y·dydx=0

⇒dydx=m2=-xy

Let θ be the angle between curve 1 & 2.
∴tanθ=m1-m21+m1m2=-b2xa2y+xy1+b2x2a2y2=xya2-b2b2x2+a2y2=xya2-b2a2b2

∴tanθa2ba+b, ab2a+b=a3b3(a+b)2·a2-b2a2b2=(a-b)ab

solution:
Parametric point on the given ellipse is 2sinθ, 3cosθ

Let, the mid-point of line segments joining -3,-5 and 2sinθ,3cosθ is h, k

Then, by using mid-point formula, we get

2sinθ-32=h, 3cosθ-52=k

⇒2sinθ=2h+3, 3cosθ=2k+5

⇒sinθ=2h+32, cosθ=2k+53

We know, sin2θ+cos2θ=1

⇒2h+322+2k+532=1

⇒144h2+9+12h+194k2+25+20k=1

⇒36h2+16k2+108h+80k+145=0

Hence, the locus of x,y is 36x2+16y2+108x+80y+145=0

solution:
Given functional equation is 

fm+n=fm+fn;m,n∈N ....1

Put m=n=3

f(3+3)=f(3)+f(3)

⇒f(3)=9∵f6=18

Put m=2,n=1 in equation 1

∴f(3)=f(2+1)=f(2)+f(1)

=f(1+1)+f(1)

=f(1)+f(1)+f(1)

9=3f(1)

⇒ 3r→-(a→+b→+c→)-r→=0→
